description: Selectors and event topics that are statistically correlated with phishing, drainer kits, or unbounded approvals. The scanner matches wallet activity against this list and surfaces any hits with high priority.

signatures.name: Infinite ERC-20 approval
signatures.description: Approval event with value = 2^256-1 (0xffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffffff). A drainer can empty the entire balance any time.
signatures.recommendation: Revoke unless the user is actively using this protocol right now.

signatures.name: Permit2 approve (Uniswap)
signatures.description: Permit2 allowances expire after a deadline but are routinely exploited via off-chain signature phishing (eth_sign / permit).
signatures.recommendation: Check expiration; revoke if not actively used.

signatures.name: Permit2 permit (off-chain signature)
signatures.description: Off-chain Permit2 signature. The user signed a message, not a tx. Drainers harvest these.
signatures.recommendation: If seen and the user did not intentionally sign, treat as compromised. Move funds.

signatures.name: ERC-2612 permit (off-chain signature)
signatures.description: Off-chain approval signature. Phishing kits love this because the user pays no gas and sees no tx in their history.
signatures.recommendation: If the user did not sign this, treat as compromised.

signatures.name: ERC-721/1155 setApprovalForAll
signatures.description: Grants a contract operator rights over ALL NFTs of a collection. Drainers target this because one signature can empty the entire wallet.
signatures.recommendation: Revoke unless actively trading on a known marketplace.

signatures.match: personal_sign / eth_sign with empty or 0x data
signatures.name: Blank eth_sign
signatures.description: Some phishing kits ask the user to sign an empty hash. The signature can authorize anything depending on the contract the user interacts with next.
signatures.recommendation: If found, the wallet is very likely compromised. Sweep funds to a new wallet.

signatures.name: ERC-20 increaseAllowance
signatures.description: Bumps an existing allowance. Less commonly used in legitimate flows; sometimes used by drainers that initially set a small allowance then bump it.
signatures.recommendation: Verify the spender. If unknown, revoke the underlying approval.

severityLevels.critical: Wallet is likely compromised. Move funds.
